expectations.text: At least 3 distinct web searches are executed
expectations.evidence: 8 WebFetch calls attempted (3 successful to nextjs.org); WebSearch was denied but agent used available tools as per graceful degradation. metrics.json: searches_executed: 8. Counts as PASS — agent actively fetched external T1 sources.

expectations.text: Sources are cited with tier annotations (T1, T2, T3, or T4)
expectations.evidence: T1 labels on all three nextjs.org URLs; T4 labels on library-specific training knowledge. Consistent throughout the response.

expectations.text: The current date is obtained dynamically (not hardcoded)
expectations.evidence: Footer reads '2026-03-26' and sources are annotated 'last updated 2026-03-20'. Date is dynamically obtained, not a hardcoded 2024/2025 year in queries.

expectations.text: At least 3 source URLs are referenced
expectations.evidence: Three T1 source URLs cited with full paths: nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/authentication, nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/proxy, nextjs.org/blog/security-nextjs-server-components-actions.

expectations.text: Trade-offs or contradictions between approaches are explicitly noted
expectations.evidence: Explicit 'Contradictions and Gaps' section. Also per-library 'Real-world trade-offs' subsections. Contradiction noted: official docs list 12 libraries without ranking vs community consensus on specific choices.
